channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Deadlocked St. Paul City Council sends interim council member decision to mayor
item.description: There were four finalists who interviewed for the seat. Three council members are in favor of one candidate, while the remaining three support another candidate.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: 5 things to know about Republicans’ new blueprint to pass Trump's agenda
item.description: Senate Republicans on Wednesday rolled out a 70-page blueprint to advance President Trump’s tax agenda, along with directions that could pave the way for more than a trillion dollars in cuts to government spending.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge questions whether EPA move to rapidly cancel 'green bank' grants was legal
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday pressed an attorney for the Environmental Protection Agency about whether the agency broke the law when it swiftly terminated $20 billion worth of grants awarded to nonprofits for a green bank by allegedly bulldozing past proper rules and raising flimsy accusations of wa
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EXCLUSIVE VIDEO: AOC refuses to say whether Democrats' rhetoric is responsible for Tesla attacks
item.description: Democrat Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez refused to answer whether Democrats' inflammatory rhetoric against Elon Musk is linked to attacks against Tesla owners and dealers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ACLU sues Trump administration over NIH grant cancellations
item.description: The lawsuit, filed in Massachusetts district court, said there was no “scientifically-valid explanation or cause” for NIH to cancel the research projects.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic states sue to stop billions in public health funding cuts
item.description: HHS said the funds, totaling $11.4 billion, were primarily used for COVID-19 response including testing, vaccination and hiring community health workers.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Trump restoring millions in family planning funds for two states
item.description: The Trump administration is restoring millions of dollars in Title X funds to two states after the Biden administration chose to withhold those funds because the pair failed to comply with program rules.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Judge dismisses corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge dismissed New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case on Wednesday, agreeing it was the only practical outcome while blasting the Justice Department’s “troubling” rationale for wanting the charges thrown out — namely so the Democrat could help President Donald Tru
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump restoring millions in family planning funds for two states
item.description: The Trump administration is restoring millions of dollars in Title X funds to Oklahoma and Tennessee after the Biden administration chose to withhold those funds because both states failed to comply with program rules.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Trump gives GOP senators his support as they charge ahead on tax package and spending cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican senators unveiled a budget proposal Wednesday that's central to President Donald Trump's domestic agenda of tax breaks, spending cuts and border security, but they're delaying some of the most difficult decisions, including how to pay for the multitrillion-dollar package
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court divided over state effort to defund Planned Parenthood
item.description: The Supreme Court is divided on whether funding for Planned Parenthood can be blocked by a state, a decision that carries the weight of a larger fight on abortion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump touts return of the 'American Dream' in historic tariff announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump announced his highly anticipated trade plan from the Rose Garden of the White House on Wednesday, which he has declared "Liberation Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court blocks DOGE effort to access Social Security data
item.description: A federal judge on March 20 temporarily blocked billionaire Elon Musk’s DOGE from Social Security systems.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court sides with the FDA in its dispute over sweet-flavored vaping products
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday ruled for the Food and Drug Administration in its crackdown on sweet-flavored vaping products following a surge in teen electronic cigarette use.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Does President Trump really need to cut down Andrew Jackson's magnolia tree? Expert weighs in
item.description: Fox News Digital consulted an expert after President Trump revealed that a historic tree at the White House, originally planted by Andrew Jackson, is set to be cut down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens arrested in major border state bust targeting bloodthirsty Venezuelan gang
item.description: Over 40 people with suspected ties to the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua were arrested by law enforcement in Hays County, Texas, on Tuesday morning, the Texas Department of Public Safety announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bitter House GOP divisions erupt after Johnson shuts down votes over Republican mutiny
item.description: House Republicans are still at odds after GOP leaders suffered an embarrassing defeat on the House floor by members of their own party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisconsin and Florida elections provide early warning signs to Trump and Republicans
item.description: A trio of spring elections offered early warning signs to Republicans and President Donald Trump, as Democrats rallied against his efforts to slash the federal government and the outsize role being played by billionaire Elon Musk in the early days of his new administration.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MAYA SWEEDLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk not leaving yet, wrapping up work on schedule once 'incredible work at DOGE is complete': White House
item.description: Elon Musk will likely wrap up his work on the Department of Government Efficiency at the end of May due to his employment as a "special government employee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: New analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Auditor reports that Minnesota muni liquor store profits up, in part due to THC offerings
item.description: Minnesota State Auditor Julie Blaha issued her annual report on municipal-run liquor store sales. It was the first include sales of hemp-derived THC beverages and gummies, and operators suggested those items contributed to store profits in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Outrage grows over Maryland man's mistaken deportation to El Salvador prison
item.description: In the 22 days since Maryland resident Kilmar Abrego Garcia was mistakenly deported to a notoriously violent prison in El Salvador, his young autistic son has sought comfort in the scent of his missing father’s clothes.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No bathroom breaks: How Booker accomplished record Senate speech
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) set a new record for the longest Senate speech in history on Monday and Tuesday by holding the upper chamber’s floor for more than 25 hours.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate rebukes Trump’s tariffs as some Republicans vote to halt taxes on Canadian imports
item.description: The Senate has passed a resolution that would thwart President Donald Trump’s ability to impose tariffs on Canada.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 takeaways from Wisconsin Supreme Court race, Florida special elections
item.description: Democrats scored a critical win Tuesday in their first major test at the ballot box since President Trump took office in January.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams federal bribery case dismissed
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams' federal bribery case has been dismissed, court records show. In a letter filed Wednesday, Judge Dale Ho ordered the case dropped with prejudice, meaning the government cannot bring these charges against Adams again in the future.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump unveils 'Liberation Day' tariffs, raising trade tensions
item.description: The tariffs goals include reducing the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. and boosting American-made manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats' win in Wisconsin court race a big loss for Elon Musk
item.description: Musk and his affiliated groups sunk at least $21 million into the normally low-profile race and paid three individual voters $1 million each for signing a petition in an effort to goose turnout in the pivotal battleground state contest.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces sweeping new tariffs to promote US manufacturing, risking inflation and trade wars
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced far-reaching new tariffs on nearly all U.S. trading partners — including a 34% tax on imports from China and 20% on the European Union, among others.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: State Sen. Napoleon Harris wins Thornton Township supervisor seat, Jason House victorious in Dolton mayoral race
item.description: Village of Dolton Senior Trustee Jason House won the election for Dolton mayor by a landslide, while State Sen. Napoleon B. Harris III claimed victory to become the Thornton Township Supervisor-elect, unseating the incumbent, self-proclaimed “Super Mayor” Tiffany Henyard.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, WGN 9

item.title: CCSD opposes school choice bill, while lawmakers say it empowers families
item.description: The Clark County School District is against a bill that would let parents enroll their children at any public school regardless of where they live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republican state Sen. Randy Fine wins special election for Florida's 6th Congressional District
item.description: Republican state Sen. Randy Fine won a special election Tuesday to represent Florida's 6th Congressional District, defeating Democratic challenger Josh Weil and squashing efforts by national Democrats who spent millions of dollars on the race.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Department of Health announces layoffs amid federal funding cuts
item.description: The agency issued layoffs and separation notices to 170 employees whose positions were supported by federal grants that have now been terminated.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Legal, medical cannabis fix-it legislation in the works at Minnesota Legislature
item.description: Sen. Scott Dibble, DFL-Minneapolis, is sponsoring several of those cleanup bills for both recreational cannabis and changes to medical marijuana rules. He joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer ahead of committee hearings Thursday to talk about what the proposed alterations entail.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5M, open to paternity test
item.description: “I don’t know if the child is mine or not, but am not against finding out. No court order is needed,” Musk said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Election Day: Suburban voters head to the polls
item.description: Several elections will take place in Chicago-area suburbs on Tuesday, including a notable one in Dolton where incumbent Tiffany Henyard will run against Jason House, who won the primary election in February.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9

item.title: Entire staff at federal agency that funds libraries and museums put on leave
item.description: The staff of the Institute of Museum and Library Services was placed on administrative leave Monday morning, following a meeting between IMLS leadership and DOGE staff.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Limbong, MPR News
